semanticCore: Demolishing, tearing down, pulling down buildings, destruction, or old worn garments

keyDerivations.meaning: To demolish, pull down, tear down structures, or level buildings

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ اللَّهِ النَّاسَ بَعْضَهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ لَهُدِّمَتْ صَوَامِعُ وَبِيَعٌ (22:40): And if it were not for God repelling people, some by means of others, monasteries and churches would have been demolished.
discardedNoise: Hadm (digesting food) - confused spelling H-D-M vs. H-D'-M.
summaryEn: The root means tearing down or demolishing structures (Hadm), stating that religious sanctuaries of various faiths would be systematically leveled without defensive resistance.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى إسقاط البناء ونقضه وتخريب الجدران والبيوت (هدم)، مبدياً أن غياب قوة الدفاع كان سيؤدي لتخريب وهدم أديرة العبادة ومساجد الصلوات.
rawText: الدَّمُ الدَّمُ وَالهَدَْمُ الهَدَْمُ : see دَمْ. اِنْهَدَمَ 7 It became thrown down, pulled down, pulled to pieces, demolished, or destroyed: and it fell in ruins, or to pieces; or became a ruin. === اِنْهَدَمَ الحَائِطُ مِنْ مَكَانِهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ هَدْمٌ [The wall fell in ruins, or to pieces, from its place, without being pulled to pieces]. (Lth, in TA, art. قيض.) تَهَادَمَتِ الحِيطَانُ 6 [The walls fell to ruin by degrees]. (S, in art. دعو.) هَدَمٌ Earth that is dug from a pit or well: see حَفَرٌ. هَدَمَ 1 He threw down, or pulled down, a building; (Msb;) pulled it to pieces; demolished it; destroyed it: (K:) [the last two explanations are the most correct, as is shown by the phrase] نَقَضَ البِنَآءَ مِنْ عَيْرِ هَدْمِ [He took to pieces the building without demolishing, or destroying]: (S, A, Msb, K, * in art. قوض:) he ruined [a building, &c.]; reduced [it] to ruin. (Ham, p. 31.)
